person.name: Jean Ier de Lévis-Mirepoix
person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
Jean Ier de Lévis-Mirepoix

Seigneur de Mirepoix (09) et de Montségur (09), Maréchal de la Foi
Né en 1271 à Mirepoix
Mariage : le 2 févr. 1296 avec Constance II de Foix
Carcassonne, 11069, Aude, Languedoc-Roussillon, France

Décès le 21 févr. 1319
Inhumation le 1 mars 1319 à Mirepoix, chapelle des Cordeliers
Sources : Famille: H&G 182 p. 70: Edouard de Saint Phalle.
relevé 2023 JPR Teyssier Chalancon

zh.lifeSummary: 當Jean Ier de Lévis-Mirepoix於1271年出生在法國奧其坦尼格斯米波瓦時，他的父親Guy III de Lévis-Mirepoix23歲，母親Isabeau de Marly de Montmorency28歲。他於1296年3月1日與Constance DE FOIX在法國奧德卡爾卡頌結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1319年2月21日在法國奧其坦尼奧德卡爾卡頌享年48歲，埋葬在法國奧其坦尼阿利艾米波瓦。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Jean Ier de Lévis-Mirepoix于1271年出生在法国奧其坦尼格斯米波瓦时，他的父亲Guy III de Lévis-Mirepoix23岁，母亲Isabeau de Marly de Montmorency28岁。他于1296年3月1日与Constance DE FOIX在法国奧德卡爾卡頌结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1319年2月21日在法国奧其坦尼奧德卡爾卡頌享年48岁，埋葬在法国奧其坦尼阿利艾米波瓦。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Jean
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Like Jane and Joan , a medieval variant of Old French Je(h)anne. Towards the end of the Middle Ages this form became largely confined to Scotland. In the 20th century it became more widely used in the English-speaking world and enjoyed a period of great popularity, but it is now out of fashion. Among numerous well-known and influential bearers are the British novelists Jean Plaidy ( Eleanor Hibbert , 1910–93 ) and Jean Rhys ( Ella Gwendolen Rees Williams , 1894–1979 ), British actress Jean Simmons ( 1929–2010 ), and American-born actress Jean Seberg ( 1938–79 ). It is also found as a variant spelling of the masculine name Gene .

variants.name: John
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

nameMeanings.name: Ier
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: and Norwegian vernacular form of   Peter .

variants.name: Peter
meanings: form of the name of the best-known of all Christ's apostles, traditionally regarded as the founder of the Christian Church. The name derives, via Latin, from Greek petros ‘stone, rock’. This is used as a translation of the Aramaic byname Cephas, given to the apostle Simon son of Jona, to distinguish him from another of the same name ( Simon Zelotes ). ‘When Jesus beheld him, he said, Thou art Simon the son of Jona: thou shalt be called Cephas, which is by interpretation, A stone’ (John 1:42). According to Matthew 16:17–18, Christ says more explicitly, ‘Blessed art thou, Simon Bar-jona … thou art Peter, and upon this rock I will build my church’. The name has been in continuous use since the Middle Ages.

en.lifeSummary: When Jean Ier de Lévis-Mirepoix was born in 1271, in Mirepoix, Gers, Occitanie, France, his father, Guy III de Lévis-Mirepoix, was 23 and his mother, Isabeau de Marly de Montmorency, was 28. He married Constance DE FOIX on 1 March 1296, in Carcassonne, Aude, France. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 21 February 1319, in Carcassonne, Aude, Occitanie, France, at the age of 48, and was buried in Mirepoix, Ariège, Occitanie, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jean Ier de Lévis-Mirepoix est né en 1271, à Mirepoix, Gers, Occitanie, France, son père, Guy III de Lévis-Mirepoix, avait 23 ans et sa mère, Isabeau de Marly de Montmorency, avait 28 ans. Il a épousé Constance DE FOIX le 1 mars 1296, à Carcassonne, Aude, France. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 21 février 1319, à Carcassonne, Aude, Occitanie, France, à l’âge de 48 ans, et a été enterré à Mirepoix, Ariège, Occitanie, France.

de.lifeSummary: Als Jean Ier de Lévis-Mirepoix 1271 in Mirepoix, Gers, Occitanie, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Guy III de Lévis-Mirepoix 23 und seine Mutter Isabeau de Marly de Montmorency 28. Er hat am 1. März 1296 in Carcassonne, Aude, Frankreich Constance DE FOIX geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 21. Februar 1319 im Alter von 48 Jahren in Carcassonne, Aude, Occitanie, Frankreich und wurde in Mirepoix, Ariège, Occitanie, Frankreich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Jean Ier de Lévis-Mirepoix è nato il nel 1271 nel Mirepoix, Gers, Occitanie, Francia, il suo padre, Guy III de Lévis-Mirepoix, aveva 23 anni e il suo madre, Isabeau de Marly de Montmorency, aveva 28 anni. Lui sposato Constance DE FOIX il 1 marzo 1296, nel Carcassonne, Aude, Francia. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 21 febbraio 1319, nel Carcassonne, Aude, Occitanie, Francia, all'età di 48 anni, e fu sepolto a Mirepoix, Ariège, Occitanie, Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: Jean Ier de Lévis-Mirepoixが1271年にフランスオクシタニージャーズミレプオイックスで生まれた時，父Guy III de Lévis-Mirepoixは23歳，母Isabeau de Marly de Montmorencyは28歳であった。1296年3月1日にフランスアウドカーカソンでConstance DE FOIXと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1319年2月21日にフランスオクシタニーアウドカーカソンで48歳で死去し，フランスオクシタニーアリeッジミレプオイックスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Jean Ier de Lévis-Mirepoix(이)가 1271년 프랑스 옥시탠이 그르스 미어프이스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Guy III de Lévis-Mirepoix(은)는 23세였고, 그의 어머니 Isabeau de Marly de Montmorency(은)는 28세였다. 그는 1296년 3월 1일 프랑스 오드 카르캐손에서 Constance DE FOIX(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1319년 2월 21일 프랑스 옥시탠이 오드 카르캐손에서 48세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 옥시탠이 애리e지 미어프이스에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Jean Ier de Lévis-Mirepoix nasceu em 1271, em Mirepoix, Gers, Occitanie, França, seu pai, Guy III de Lévis-Mirepoix, tinha 23 anos e sua mãe, Isabeau de Marly de Montmorency, tinha 28 anos. Ele casou-se com Constance DE FOIX em 1 de março de 1296, em Carcassonne, Aude, França. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 21 de fevereiro de 1319, em Carcassonne, Aude, Occitanie, França, com 48 anos, e foi sepultado em Mirepoix, Ariège, Occitanie, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jean Ier de Lévis-Mirepoix родился в 1271 г. в местности мирепоикс, джирс, окситани, Франция, его отцу по имени Guy III de Lévis-Mirepoix было 23 года, а матери, которую звали Isabeau de Marly de Montmorency, 28. Он женился на женщине по имени Constance DE FOIX 1 марта 1296 г. в местности каркассонн, ауд, Франция. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер 21 февраля 1319 г. в местности каркассонн, ауд, окситани, Франция в возрасте 48 лет и был похоронен в местности мирепоикс, ариeдж, окситани, Франция.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Jean Ier de Lévis-Mirepoix nació en 1271, en Mirepoix, Gers, Occitanie, Francia, su papá, Guy III de Lévis-Mirepoix, tenía 23 años y su mamá, Isabeau de Marly de Montmorency, tenía 28 años. Se casó con Constance DE FOIX el 1 de marzo de 1296, en Carcassonne, Aude, Francia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija. Murió el 21 de febrero de 1319, en Carcassonne, Aude, Occitanie, Francia, a la edad de 48 años, y fue sepultado en Mirepoix, Ariège, Occitanie, Francia.
